steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for an opening to a narration about Wulffia maculata’s collection. The opening must focus on that specific specimen — not comparisons with other plants or general information about Mexía’s broader work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It was on January 25, 1930, in the state of Minas Gerais, that Ynés Mexía added a new specimen to her growing collection of botanical samples: *Wulffia maculata* of the Asteraceae family.: gives the date, location, and family classification, anchoring that specimen as the clear center of the narrative. ✓
  • Choice B — Ynés Mexía collected a sample of *Wulffia maculata* in January of 1930 and *Senecio chapalensis* in January of 1927.: shifts focus away from Wulffia maculata to a different plant. ✗
  • Choice C — While both specimens collected by Ynés Mexía are members of the Asteraceae family, *Wulffia maculata* was collected in Minas Gerais, Brazil, and *Senecio chapalensis* was collected in Jalisco, Mexico.: makes another plant the reference point rather than centering Wulffia maculata. ✗
  • Choice D — Thousands of botanical samples collected from across the Americas by Ynés Mexía can now be found in one place: online at the C. V. Starr Virtual Herbarium.: moves attention to present-day storage rather than the moment of collection. ✗
That matches Choice A — It was on January 25, 1930, in the state of Minas Gerais, that Ynés Mexía added a new specimen to her growing collection of botanical samples: *Wulffia maculata* of the Asteraceae family..
